content.full_text:
FPPC
JAN 15 8 CO AH '90

January 11, 1990


Kathryn Donovan
Legal Division Fair Political Practice Commission
428 J Street, #800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Ms. Donovan:

I am writing you in regards to the city of Carson's concern over how, or if, Proposition 78 would affect a cable presentation involving elected officials.

Our city plans to do a state-of-the-city type program involving our city's elected officials addressing local concerns for the benefit of community residents. However, four of the elected officials are up for reelection in April. The city runs a similar program with city staff explaining what their departments do and how they affect citizens.

I would appreciate any advice or information that you could provide the city for this type of situation. Please feel free to contact me at 213-830-7600, ext. 315 if you need any further information, or have any questions.

Thank you for your attention to this manner and I look forward to hearing from you.

Regards,

Eva Gatling
Public Information Manager

EG:EM:sb

701 EAST CARSON STREET, P.O. BOX 6234, CARSON, CALIFORNIA 90749 • PHONE (213) 830-7600

sections.question: How, or if, would Proposition 78 affect a cable presentation involving elected officials who are up for reelection in April, where the city plans to do a state-of-the-city type program with elected officials addressing local concerns?
sections.question_synthetic: The City of Carson seeks guidance on whether Proposition 78 would restrict or regulate a cable television program featuring elected officials discussing city matters when some of those officials are facing reelection.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from City of Carson to FPPC, not an FPPC response. No formal QUESTION/CONCLUSION sections present. Document is a request for advice regarding Proposition 78 compliance. Some text degradation in scanned document affects confidence slightly.
